avoid: Replacing the full W@X with a custom GEMM; cuBLAS is already optimal.
Hardcoding hidden dimensions or relying on shape-specific tuning without dispatch.
Unsafe caching by pointer or shape alone without version counters.
evidence: Aggregate speedup 0.996, essentially no improvement over baseline.
Speedup at d=3584 is 1.008 (marginal), but regresses at d=4096 (0.993) and d=4608 (0.988).
Custom rank-16 update kernel uses scalar loads/stores (vector_width=1) despite all d being divisible by 4, missing memory bandwidth optimization.
Block size 128 may not be optimal for memory-bound kernel; no evidence of tuning.
recommended_actions: Use vectorized loads/stores (float4) for the rank-16 update to improve memory throughput.
Experiment with block sizes (e.g., 256, 512) to find better occupancy and latency hiding.
Ensure memory coalescing by having threads access contiguous elements along the d dimension.
Consider fusing the addition of W@X with the rank-16 update in a single kernel to reduce memory traffic, but ensure vectorization.
